# Pingpong â€” R1 (In-Flight)

> **Bout:** pingpong-2026-05-02-R1
> **Target:** 10.129.30.247 (HTB Pingpong)
> **Outcome:** âŹł **incomplete** â€” no `autopilot.finish`, in-flight or aborted
> **Studied:** 2026-05-02 (later)

## The fight

The bell has rung but the punches haven't connected. Pingpong is a
new box, fresh entry on the card, and R1's audit tape stops
mid-stride: 176 events, no `autopilot.finish`, no flags, no clean
verdict. Could be the operator killed the run between rounds; could
be it's still in the corner; could be it crashed quietly. **Tatum
stays patient.** The next entry on this tape will tell the story
when the bout finishes.

What we know is what's already in `~/HTB/machines/pingpong/out-r1/`.
That's plenty to log; not enough to call.

## Tale of the tape

| stat | pingpong-R1 |
| ---- | ----------- |
| target | `10.129.30.247` (HTB Pingpong) |
| audit events | **176** (operator pulse) / 219 lines on disk |
| `azure_openai.agent.chat.start` | **14** (operator pulse) / 19 in audit |
| `http_probe` calls | **11** (operator pulse) / 17 `http.start` in audit |
| `http.error` events | 14 |
| `execute_cred_spray` | 5 (4 `password-spray` cycles fired) |
| `run_multi_stage` | 3 (`multistage.preflight`/`poc`/`chain` start+finish triplets) |
| `exploit_plan` | 2 (per pulse: 1 â€” pulse rounds may differ) |

| `tls_cipher_enum` | 1 |
| `ldap_probe` | 2 |
| budget denials | **3** (`budget.deny`) |
| `autopilot.finish` | **0** |
| flags | none reported |

| last event | `2026-05-02T07:14:02Z` (`http.start` mid-flight) |
| run window | ~9 minutes from session.start to last write |

Operator's canonical pulse: **176 / 14 / 11 / 5 / 2 / 1 / 0 finish**.
The on-disk audit shows slightly higher counts on the verbose
events (chat starts double for start/finish pairs; some `http.start`
entries are direct calls outside `http_probe`). Either way the
shape of the fight is the same: planner is engaged, recon is firing,
multi-stage is reaching the PoC step, but no kill blow has landed
and no clean exit was recorded.

## What Drederick has so far

Tools that fired before the tape stopped:

- **Recon:** `http_probe` (Ă—11 per pulse), `tls_probe` (Ă—1),
  `tls_cipher_enum` (Ă—1), `ldap_probe` (Ă—2). HTTPS surface on
  `:8443` confirmed alive in the last recorded event.
- **Exploit planning:** `exploit_plan` (Ă—1 per pulse) and
  `run_multi_stage` (Ă—3) â€” the planner reached the PoC stage and
  invoked `msf-rc` (Ă—2). That means the GAP-033 cve-lead router
  *did* find something to fetch on this box; whether it succeeded
  is downstream of `msf-rc.finish` which we have but no
  `multistage.chain` outcome to call.
- **Credential attacks:** `execute_cred_spray` (Ă—5) wrapping
  `password-spray` (Ă—4) â€” sprays cycled with one preflight short.
- **Doctor:** 32 `doctor.detect` events at session start (preflight
  ran clean).
- **Budget pressure:** 3 `budget.deny` events â€” the budget knobs are
  biting on a hard or busy surface.

No `autopilot.finish` means no `findings.db` summary to read off
yet â€” the run never reached the report step. Conclusions on
services/CVEs/flags wait for the next entry.

## Holding the corner

Pingpong is the **test bed for whether the in-flight fixes carry
over to a new box**. The harness arc just shipped (or is shipping)
four pieces that all need a fresh fight to validate:

- **`llm-exec-shell-tool`** â€” does the LLM start using it on
  pingpong, or fall back to the same recon+spray loop that lost
  on Facts R1-R4?
- **`gap-031b-2-git-poc-sources`** â€” when the cve-lead router
  invokes `FetchOnDemandAsync` on pingpong's CVEs, does the
  git-clone source actually return artifacts? Watch
  `multistage.poc` and `poc.fetch` event counts.
- **`gap-032b-spray-vhost-aware`** â€” pingpong probably has its
  own vhost; does `execute_cred_spray` carry the Host header
  this round, or do all 5 sprays go to the IP the way they did
  on Facts R3+R4?
- **`gap-034-http-error-taxonomy`** â€” pingpong already produced
  14 `http.error` events. Once the taxonomy lands, the next
  read of this tape can tell `connection_refused` from
  `host_header_rejected` from `redirect_loop` in one query
  instead of a manual `tail | grep`.

The signals to watch when the fight resumes (or the next round
opens): `multistage.chain.finish` outcome, `poc.fetch` count > 0,
`execute_cred_spray` argv carrying `Host:` headers, `http.error`
events with a `reason` field. Any of those firing is a
production-tape credit on a fix that landed.

## Records & residue

- **First fight on the new box.** Pingpong joins the card.
- **Provider note:** R1 ran on `azure_openai`, not Copilot â€”
  worth flagging because most of the recent tape is Copilot SDK.
  Cross-provider regression coverage is a quiet win.
- **No call.** Outcome `incomplete` â€” the next entry will tell
  the story.

â€” *Filed from ringside, 2026-05-02 (later). The bell will ring again.*
